findings.description: Auth code needed defense-in-depth: localhost binding, io.LimitReader, HTTPS enforcement, Fstat on open fd, filepath.Clean. Each individually low risk but collectively significant.
findings.proposedRule: Auth-adjacent code applies defense-in-depth: bind listeners to 127.0.0.1, limit I/O reads, enforce HTTPS, use Fstat, sanitize paths

findings.source: plan.md as-built notes (Login, Refresh, Resolver signatures changed)
findings.description: All three major functions needed signature changes from plan to support testability. Config structs with optional fields solved it without API churn.
findings.proposedRule: Use config structs with optional fields for functions requiring test injection

findings.source: 8 WARNs resolved in single commit before PR
findings.description: All WARNs were small fixes (1-5 lines). Fixing immediately post-verify was cheap with fresh context. Deferred WARNs would have become tech debt.
findings.proposedRule: Fix all WARN-level gate findings before shipping while context is fresh

findings.source: wiring WARN #3 — three duplicate keysOf/jsonKeys/rawKeys helpers
findings.description: Three test files independently wrote identical map-keys helpers with different names. Consolidated into testhelpers_test.go.
findings.proposedRule: When 2+ test files need the same helper, put it in testhelpers_test.go immediately

findings.source: Keyring interface pattern, oauth2.HTTPClient context key
findings.description: Keyring interface let us defer go-keyring dependency while fully testing. oauth2.HTTPClient context key propagated custom clients without API changes.
findings.proposedRule: Define interfaces for external deps at consumer site; wire real implementations at outermost layer
